title: The Salesman
learningGoal: The learner will verbally construct fluid noun phrases (Adjective + Noun) without stammering, successfully applying lexical stress to the adjective to emulate native expression during a persuasive dialogue.
instructions: You are a salesman trying to sell a house to a family! They will ask you questions about the house. You must answer them by describing the house and the neighborhood using adjectives. Put a little extra energy into the describing words!

contentItems.text: Remember: NEVER put an 's' on the describing word. And say the describing word slightly louder than the noun!

speakingPrompt: Respond to the customer's Hindi questions in English.
scenario: A family is looking at a house you are trying to sell them.

subTasks.prompt: ग्राहक: 'Is this a small house?' (क्या यह एक छोटा घर है?)। जवाब दें: 'नहीं, यह एक बड़ा घर है।' (A big house).

sampleResponse.targetText: No, it is a big house.
sampleResponse.transliteration: नो, इट इज़ अ बिग हाउस।
sampleResponse.baseTranslation: नहीं, यह एक बड़ा घर है।

subTasks.prompt: ग्राहक: 'What about the kitchen?' (रसोई के बारे में क्या?)। जवाब दें: 'रसोई साफ और सुंदर है।' (clean and beautiful).

sampleResponse.targetText: The kitchen is clean and beautiful.
sampleResponse.transliteration: द किचन इज़ क्लीन एंड ब्यूटीफुल।

subTasks.prompt: ग्राहक: 'Is the street loud?' (क्या सड़क पर शोर है?)। जवाब दें: 'नहीं, यह एक शांत सड़क है।' (A quiet street).

sampleResponse.targetText: No, it is a quiet street.
sampleResponse.transliteration: नो, इट इज़ अ क्वाइट स्ट्रीट।
sampleResponse.baseTranslation: नहीं, यह एक शांत सड़क है।

subTasks.prompt: ग्राहक: 'Are the schools bad?' (क्या स्कूल बुरे हैं?)। जवाब दें: 'नहीं, वे अच्छे स्कूल हैं!' (Good schools - No 's' on good!)
ग्राहक: 'I like it. I am very happy.' (मुझे यह पसंद है। मैं बहुत खुश हूँ।)। जवाब दें: 'मैं भी खुश हूँ!' (I am... too).

scenarioContext.baseTranslation: क्या यह एक छोटा घर है?

baselineTranscriptSentences.targetText: No, it is a big house.
baselineTranscriptSentences.transliteration: नो, इट इज़ अ बिग हाउस।
baselineTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: नहीं, यह एक बड़ा घर है।

baselineTranscriptSentences.targetText: The kitchen is clean and beautiful.
baselineTranscriptSentences.transliteration: द किचन इज़ क्लीन एंड ब्यूटीफुल।

baselineTranscriptSentences.targetText: No, it is a quiet street.
baselineTranscriptSentences.transliteration: नो, इट इज़ अ क्वाइट स्ट्रीट।
baselineTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: नहीं, यह एक शांत सड़क है।

scoringRubric.Adjective Placement: Did the user say 'A big house' and NOT 'A house big'?
scoringRubric.No Plurals on Adjectives: Did the user correctly say 'Good schools' and NOT 'Goods schools'?
scoringRubric.Lexical Stress: Did the user place slightly more vocal emphasis on the modifiers?

adminCorrectAnswerSet.sampleAnswer: No, it is a big house. The kitchen is clean and beautiful. No, it is a quiet street. No, they are good schools. I am happy too!

feedbackRules.onPass: Magnificent! You successfully sold the house by using fluid, perfectly placed English adjectives. Best of all, you didn't say 'goods schools'!
feedbackRules.onFail: Keep practicing! You probably put the describing word in the wrong place. Say 'A BIG HOUSE'. Just like in Hindi (Bada Ghar), the describing word comes FIRST.
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good try! Remember, when talking about multiple things (Schools), the noun gets an 'S', but the describing word (Good) NEVER gets an 's'!

audioAssets.audioTranscript: Customer: Is this a small house?

hintText: To sound very native, stretch the describing word just a tiny bit. 'It is a biiiiiig house.'
commonMistakes: Stammering between the article and the adjective ('A... uh... big house').
